name: Question Bank a Reverfiez dans le PDF
description: SQL, NoSQL, data modeling and database management

questions.question: A company collects 500 GB of sensor data per day from each global site and needs to aggregate it as quickly as possible into one S3 bucket with minimal operational complexity. Which solution should the company choose?
choices: Turn on S3 Transfer Acceleration on the destination S3 bucket and use multipart uploads from each site directly to that bucket.
Upload to a nearby regional S3 bucket first, then use S3 Cross-Region Replication to a destination bucket and delete source data.
Use daily AWS Snowball Edge jobs from each site, then replicate to the destination bucket.
Upload to EC2 + EBS in nearby Regions, snapshot and copy to destination Region, then restore.
questions.explanation: S3 Transfer Acceleration with multipart upload is the fastest and simplest internet-based method for globally distributed high-throughput uploads directly into a single destination bucket.

questions.question: A company stores JSON application logs in S3 and needs simple on-demand SQL analysis with minimal architecture changes and operational overhead. What should be used?
choices: Load logs into Amazon Redshift and run SQL queries.
Move logs to Amazon CloudWatch Logs and query there.
Use Amazon Athena directly on the S3 data.
Use AWS Glue catalog + transient EMR Spark clusters for SQL.
questions.explanation: Athena is serverless, queries data in-place in S3, and requires minimal operational effort for ad hoc SQL.

questions.question: An S3 bucket in the AWS Organizations management account must be accessible only by principals from accounts in the same organization. What is the least operationally complex approach?
choices: Add the aws:PrincipalOrgID global condition key (with the organization ID) in the bucket policy.
Create OUs per department and use aws:PrincipalOrgPaths in bucket policy.
Use CloudTrail events to detect org membership changes and continuously update the bucket policy.
Tag users and enforce access with aws:PrincipalTag in bucket policy.
questions.explanation: Using aws:PrincipalOrgID in the bucket policy is the most direct and low-maintenance way to restrict access to principals within one organization.

questions.question: An EC2 instance in a VPC must access an S3 bucket privately without internet connectivity. Which solution provides this private connectivity?
choices: Create a gateway VPC endpoint for Amazon S3.
Stream logs to CloudWatch Logs and export to S3.
Create an instance profile to allow S3 access.
Create a private API Gateway API to access S3.
questions.explanation: A gateway VPC endpoint for S3 enables private access from the VPC to S3 over the AWS network without using the internet.

questions.question: A web app runs on two EC2 instances in different AZs behind an ALB, each with its own EBS volume for uploaded documents. Users only see part of their documents depending on refresh. What should be done?
choices: Copy data so both EBS volumes always contain all documents.
Configure ALB to direct each user to the server with their documents.
Copy data from both EBS volumes to Amazon EFS and update the app to store new documents in EFS.
Configure ALB to send every request to both servers and merge results.
questions.explanation: EBS is instance-scoped and not shared across instances. Amazon EFS provides shared, multi-AZ file storage so all instances see the same document set.

questions.question: A company has 70 TB of on-prem NFS video data (1 MB to 500 GB files), no longer growing, and must migrate to S3 as quickly as possible using the least network bandwidth. Which solution fits best?
choices: Use AWS CLI over the network to copy files directly to S3.
Use AWS Snowball Edge, transfer data locally to the device, and return it for import into S3.
Deploy S3 File Gateway and copy data over the network to S3.
Set up Direct Connect + S3 File Gateway and transfer over that link.
questions.explanation: Snowball Edge is designed for large one-time migrations, minimizes network usage, and can accelerate transfer of tens of TBs.

questions.question: An ingestion app receives highly variable traffic spikes up to 100,000 messages per second. Dozens of consumers need to process messages quickly. The company wants decoupling and scalability. Which architecture is best?
choices: Persist messages in Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics and let consumers read from it.
Scale EC2 ingestion instances by CPU in an Auto Scaling group.
Write to Kinesis Data Streams with one shard, preprocess with Lambda, then consumers read from DynamoDB.
Publish to an Amazon SNS topic with multiple Amazon SQS subscriptions, and let each consumer process from its queue.
questions.explanation: SNS fanout to multiple SQS queues decouples producers/consumers and scales well for many downstream services with independent processing.

questions.question: A distributed legacy app has a primary server coordinating jobs across compute nodes. The company wants a modern AWS design maximizing resiliency and scalability for variable workloads. What should be used?
choices: Send jobs to SQS, run workers on EC2 Auto Scaling, and use scheduled scaling.
Send jobs to SQS, run workers on EC2 Auto Scaling, and scale based on queue depth.
Keep primary + workers on EC2 Auto Scaling and use CloudTrail as the job destination.
Keep primary + workers on EC2 Auto Scaling and use EventBridge as the job destination, scaling on worker load.
questions.explanation: An SQS-based worker pattern removes tight coupling and queue-depth-based Auto Scaling aligns capacity directly with backlog, improving resiliency and scalability.

questions.question: A company has an on-prem SMB file server. Files are frequently accessed for 7 days, then rarely accessed. Storage is near capacity, and the company needs low-latency access for recent files plus lifecycle management. Which solution is best?
choices: Use DataSync to copy data older than 7 days to AWS.
Use Amazon S3 File Gateway and configure S3 Lifecycle to transition to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 7 days.
Create Amazon FSx for Windows File Server to extend storage.
Install per-user utilities to access S3 and transition to Glacier Flexible Retrieval after 7 days.
questions.explanation: S3 File Gateway provides on-prem file access with local cache for recent data, while S3 Lifecycle automates tiering of older objects to lower-cost storage.

questions.question: An ecommerce app sends new orders through API Gateway and requires orders to be processed strictly in the order received. Which solution meets this requirement?
choices: API Gateway publishes to SNS, Lambda subscriber processes orders.
API Gateway sends to an SQS FIFO queue, which triggers Lambda for processing.
Use an API Gateway authorizer to block requests while each order processes.
API Gateway sends to an SQS standard queue, which triggers Lambda for processing.
questions.explanation: SQS FIFO preserves strict ordering and exactly-once processing semantics (with proper configuration), making it suitable for ordered order processing.

questions.question: A company runs an application on EC2 instances with an Amazon Aurora database. Database usernames and passwords are stored locally in a file on each instance. The company wants to minimize credential management overhead. What should a solutions architect do?
choices: Use AWS Secrets Manager and enable automatic rotation.
Use AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store and enable automatic rotation.
Store an encrypted credential file in Amazon S3 and point the app to S3.
Store the credential file on encrypted EBS volumes attached to each instance.
questions.explanation: Secrets Manager provides built-in secret storage, retrieval, and automatic rotation workflows, minimizing operational overhead for database credentials.

questions.question: A global company serves static data from S3 and dynamic data from EC2 behind an ALB. The company wants lower latency and better performance for both using its Route 53 domain. What should be implemented?
choices: Create one CloudFront distribution with both S3 bucket and ALB as origins, and route Route 53 traffic to CloudFront.
Create CloudFront with ALB origin and Global Accelerator with S3 endpoint, then route Route 53 to CloudFront.
Create CloudFront with S3 origin and Global Accelerator with ALB + CloudFront endpoints, then use custom domain to accelerator.
Create CloudFront with ALB origin and Global Accelerator with S3 endpoint, then use two domain names.
questions.explanation: CloudFront can use multiple origins (S3 for static and ALB for dynamic) and provides global edge caching and acceleration with a single domain path pattern strategy.

questions.question: A company must rotate Amazon RDS for MySQL credentials monthly across multiple AWS Regions with the least operational overhead. Which solution should be used?
choices: Store credentials in AWS Secrets Manager, replicate secrets to required Regions, and configure scheduled rotation.
Store credentials as secure strings in Systems Manager with multi-Region replication and scheduled rotation.
Store encrypted credentials in S3 and trigger Lambda with EventBridge to rotate.
Store credentials in DynamoDB global tables encrypted with KMS multi-Region keys and rotate using Lambda + RDS API.
questions.explanation: Secrets Manager natively supports secret rotation and multi-Region replication, giving the lowest operational overhead for cross-Region DB credential management.

questions.question: An ecommerce application on EC2 Auto Scaling uses a MySQL database on a single large EC2 instance. Read traffic is much higher than write traffic, and read demand is unpredictable. The company needs automatic scaling and high availability. Which solution fits best?
choices: Use Amazon Redshift single-node deployment.
Use Amazon RDS Single-AZ and add reader instances in another AZ.
Use Amazon Aurora Multi-AZ and configure Aurora Auto Scaling for Aurora Replicas.
Use ElastiCache for Memcached on Spot Instances.
questions.explanation: Aurora supports high availability and read scaling through Aurora Replicas, and Aurora Auto Scaling adjusts replica count automatically for variable read workloads.

questions.question: A company needs in-cloud traffic inspection and filtering for production VPC ingress/egress, similar to its on-prem inspection server. Which AWS service should be used?
choices: Amazon GuardDuty
VPC Traffic Mirroring
AWS Network Firewall
AWS Firewall Manager
questions.explanation: AWS Network Firewall is the managed service designed for VPC traffic inspection and filtering through configurable stateful and stateless rules.

questions.question: A data lake includes data in S3 and Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL. The company needs visual dashboards for all sources, with full access for management and limited access for other employees. Which solution meets this requirement?
choices: Use QuickSight, publish dashboards, and share with IAM roles.
Use QuickSight, publish dashboards, and share with QuickSight users and groups.
Use Glue crawlers and ETL jobs to generate reports in S3; control access with S3 bucket policies.
Use Glue + Athena Federated Query, generate S3 reports, and control access with S3 bucket policies.
questions.explanation: Amazon QuickSight is purpose-built for BI dashboards and supports fine-grained sharing through QuickSight users and groups for role-based visibility.

questions.question: A new application runs on two EC2 instances and stores documents in S3. How should access from EC2 to S3 be granted?
choices: Create an IAM role with S3 permissions and attach it to the EC2 instances.
Create an IAM policy with S3 permissions and attach it directly to the EC2 instances.
Create an IAM group with S3 permissions and attach it to the EC2 instances.
Create an IAM user with S3 permissions and attach that user to the EC2 instances.
questions.explanation: EC2 instances should receive AWS permissions through an IAM role (instance profile), which avoids static credentials and follows AWS best practice.

questions.question: A microservice must process images uploaded to S3, compress them with Lambda, and store results in another S3 bucket using durable stateless components. Which two actions should be taken?
choices: Configure S3 upload notifications to send messages to an SQS queue.
Configure Lambda to consume messages from SQS and delete messages after successful processing.
Have Lambda monitor S3 and track processed files in memory.
Run an EC2 instance to poll SQS and invoke Lambda while tracking files locally.
Use EventBridge to send an SNS email alert for manual processing.
questions.explanation: S3 event notifications to SQS plus Lambda event source mapping provides an asynchronous, durable, and stateless processing pipeline.

questions.question: A company needs to inspect all inbound traffic to web servers by integrating a third-party firewall appliance in an inspection VPC with least operational overhead. Which design is best?
choices: Use a Network Load Balancer in the application VPC public subnet.
Use an Application Load Balancer in the application VPC public subnet.
Use a transit gateway in the inspection VPC and custom routes.
Use a Gateway Load Balancer in the inspection VPC with Gateway Load Balancer endpoints.
questions.explanation: Gateway Load Balancer is built to deploy and scale virtual network appliances transparently using GENEVE and GWLB endpoints.

questions.question: A company needs to clone large EBS-backed production data into test quickly in the same Region. Test changes must not affect production, and high I/O is required. What solution minimizes clone time?
choices: Restore production snapshots onto EC2 instance store volumes.
Use EBS Multi-Attach and attach production volumes to test instances.
Create and initialize new EBS volumes first, then restore snapshots.
Enable EBS Fast Snapshot Restore on snapshots, then restore to new EBS volumes and attach to test instances.
questions.explanation: Fast Snapshot Restore provides fully initialized volumes immediately from snapshots, reducing restore latency and accelerating environment cloning.

questions.question: An ecommerce company launches a one-deal-per-day site with millions of requests per hour and millisecond latency goals. Which option gives least operational overhead?
choices: Host full site in multiple S3 buckets behind CloudFront and store order data in S3.
Run full stack on EC2 Auto Scaling + ALBs and store data in RDS MySQL.
Run full stack on EKS with cluster autoscaling and store data in RDS MySQL.
Host static content in S3 behind CloudFront, use API Gateway + Lambda for backend, and store data in DynamoDB.
questions.explanation: A serverless architecture (CloudFront, S3, API Gateway, Lambda, DynamoDB) handles extreme scale with minimal operational management.

questions.question: A digital media app stores files in S3. Data must survive AZ loss, access patterns are frequent for some objects and unpredictable for others, and storage plus retrieval costs should be minimized. Which storage class is best?
choices: S3 Standard
S3 Intelligent-Tiering
S3 Standard-IA
S3 One Zone-IA
questions.explanation: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically optimizes cost across changing and unpredictable access patterns while maintaining multi-AZ resilience.

questions.question: Backup files are in S3 Standard, accessed frequently for 1 month, then never accessed but retained indefinitely. What is the most cost-effective approach?
choices: Use S3 Intelligent-Tiering.
Use an S3 Lifecycle rule to transition to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 month.
Use an S3 Lifecycle rule to transition to S3 Standard-IA after 1 month.
Use an S3 Lifecycle rule to transition to S3 One Zone-IA after 1 month.
questions.explanation: For long-term retention with essentially no access after the first month, Glacier Deep Archive provides the lowest storage cost.

questions.question: A billing team needs a graph comparing the last 2 months of EC2 costs and detailed analysis by instance type to find the root cause of vertical scaling, with least operational overhead. What should be used?
choices: AWS Budgets reports by instance type.
AWS Cost Explorer with granular filtering by instance type.
AWS Billing and Cost Management dashboard graphs only.
Cost and Usage Reports to S3 plus QuickSight dashboards.
questions.explanation: Cost Explorer provides built-in visualization and granular cost filtering with minimal setup compared to custom reporting pipelines.

questions.question: A company is designing an application that uses AWS Lambda with Amazon API Gateway to store data in an Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL database. During the proof of concept, Lambda quotas had to be increased significantly to handle high data volumes. Which design improves scalability and minimizes configuration effort?
choices: Refactor the Lambda function to run on Amazon EC2 with Apache Tomcat and JDBC
Change Aurora to Amazon DynamoDB and use DAX
Use two Lambda functions integrated with Amazon SNS
Use two Lambda functions integrated with Amazon SQS
questions.explanation: Amazon SQS decouples ingestion from database writes, smooths traffic spikes, and removes the need to scale Lambda concurrency aggressively.

questions.question: A company wants to ensure that its Amazon S3 buckets do not have unauthorized configuration changes. What should a solutions architect do?
choices: Turn on AWS Config with appropriate rules
Turn on AWS Trusted Advisor
Turn on Amazon Inspector
Enable S3 server access logging and EventBridge
questions.explanation: AWS Config tracks resource configuration changes and evaluates them against compliance rules.

questions.question: A product manager without an AWS account needs periodic access to an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard, following least privilege. What is the best solution?
choices: Share the CloudWatch dashboard using a shareable link
Create an IAM user with CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess
Create an IAM user with ViewOnlyAccess
Use a bastion host with cached credentials
questions.explanation: CloudWatch dashboards can be shared publicly without requiring an AWS account, providing least-privilege access.

questions.question: A company uses AWS Organizations and needs single sign-on across accounts while continuing to manage users in on-premises Microsoft Active Directory. Which solution meets these requirements?
choices: AWS SSO with one-way trust using AWS Managed Microsoft AD
AWS SSO with two-way trust using AWS Managed Microsoft AD
AWS Directory Service with two-way trust only
On-premises IdP with AWS SSO
questions.explanation: AWS SSO integrated with AWS Managed Microsoft AD using a two-way trust enables centralized SSO while retaining on-prem AD management.

questions.question: A VoIP service uses UDP and runs on EC2 Auto Scaling groups across multiple Regions. Users must be routed to the lowest-latency Region with automated failover. Which solution meets these requirements?
choices: Network Load Balancer with AWS Global Accelerator
Application Load Balancer with AWS Global Accelerator
Network Load Balancer with Route 53 latency routing and CloudFront
Application Load Balancer with Route 53 weighted routing and CloudFront
questions.explanation: NLB supports UDP, and AWS Global Accelerator provides latency-based routing and automatic failover.

questions.question: A team runs monthly 48-hour intensive tests on an Amazon RDS for MySQL instance. How can the team reduce cost without reducing instance size?
choices: Stop the DB instance when tests finish
Use Auto Scaling with the DB instance
Snapshot, terminate, and restore the DB instance
Downsize the DB instance between tests
questions.explanation: Stopping an RDS instance avoids compute charges while retaining storage and configuration.

questions.question: A company wants to ensure all EC2, RDS, and Redshift resources are properly tagged with minimal operational effort. What should be done?
choices: Use AWS Config rules
Use Cost Explorer
Write custom EC2 scripts
Use Lambda with scheduled API calls
questions.explanation: AWS Config rules automatically evaluate tagging compliance across resources.

questions.question: A development team needs to host a static website (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, images) at the lowest cost. What is the best solution?
choices: Host in AWS Fargate
Host in an Amazon S3 bucket
Host on an Amazon EC2 instance
Use ALB with Lambda
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 static website hosting is the most cost-effective option for static content.

questions.question: An online marketplace must stream millions of financial transactions in near real time, sanitize sensitive data, store it in a document database, and share it with multiple applications. What is the best solution?
choices: DynamoDB with DynamoDB Streams
Kinesis Data Firehose with Lambda and S3
Kinesis Data Streams with Lambda and DynamoDB
S3 batch processing with Lambda
questions.explanation: Kinesis Data Streams supports near-real-time processing, Lambda enables transformation, and DynamoDB provides low-latency access.

questions.question: A company must track configuration changes and record a history of API calls on AWS resources. What should be used?
choices: CloudTrail for config changes, AWS Config for API calls
AWS Config for config changes, CloudTrail for API calls
AWS Config and CloudWatch
CloudTrail and CloudWatch
questions.explanation: AWS Config tracks resource configuration changes, while CloudTrail logs API activity.

questions.question: A public web application runs on EC2 behind an ELB. DNS is managed by a third party. How can the company protect against large-scale DDoS attacks?
choices: Enable GuardDuty
Enable Inspector
Enable AWS Shield with Route 53
Enable AWS Shield Advanced and associate the ELB
questions.explanation: AWS Shield Advanced provides enhanced DDoS protection for ELB resources.

questions.question: A company stores data in S3 buckets in two Regions and must encrypt all data using the same customer-managed KMS key in both Regions. What solution has the least operational overhead?
choices: Use SSE-S3 with replication
Use multi-Region KMS key with client-side encryption
Use SSE-S3 with customer-managed keys
Use SSE-KMS with customer-managed keys in each Region
questions.explanation: Multi-Region KMS keys allow encryption and decryption across Regions with minimal management overhead.

questions.question: A company needs secure, repeatable remote administration of EC2 instances using native AWS services with minimal overhead. What is the best solution?
choices: Use EC2 serial console
Use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager
Deploy a bastion host
Use a Site-to-Site VPN
questions.explanation: Session Manager provides secure, auditable access without bastion hosts or SSH keys.

questions.question: A static website hosted on Amazon S3 is accessed globally and needs reduced latency at the lowest cost. What is the best solution?
choices: Replicate S3 buckets to all Regions
Use AWS Global Accelerator
Use Amazon CloudFront
Enable S3 Transfer Acceleration
questions.explanation: CloudFront caches content at edge locations, reducing latency cost-effectively.

questions.question: An RDS for MySQL database with 2 TB of General Purpose SSD storage experiences slow inserts due to storage performance. What should be done?
choices: Switch to Provisioned IOPS SSD
Use a memory-optimized instance
Use a burstable instance
Add Multi-AZ read replicas
questions.explanation: Provisioned IOPS provides consistent high-performance storage for write-intensive workloads.

questions.question: Thousands of edge devices generate 1 TB of alerts daily. The company needs a highly available, low-cost, serverless ingestion and storage solution with 14 days of hot data and archival afterward. What is the best solution?
choices: Kinesis Data Firehose to S3 with lifecycle to Glacier
EC2 ingestion layer with S3
Kinesis Firehose to OpenSearch with snapshots
Amazon SQS with consumers and S3 archiving
questions.explanation: Kinesis Data Firehose is fully managed, highly available, and integrates directly with S3 and lifecycle policies for cost-efficient archiving.

questions.question: A company's application integrates with multiple SaaS sources for data collection. EC2 instances currently receive data, upload it to S3, and send completion notifications. Performance is slow. Which solution improves performance with the least operational overhead?
choices: Use an EC2 Auto Scaling group and configure S3 event notifications to SNS.
Use Amazon AppFlow to transfer data from each SaaS source to S3 and configure S3 event notifications to SNS.
Create EventBridge rules per SaaS source to put data into S3 and a second rule to SNS on upload completion.
Containerize the app on Amazon ECS and use CloudWatch Container Insights to notify SNS.
questions.explanation: Amazon AppFlow is a managed integration service purpose-built for SaaS-to-AWS data flows and reduces operational burden compared to maintaining EC2 ingestion logic.

questions.question: A highly available EC2 image-processing application in multiple AZs downloads/uploads images to S3 through a single NAT gateway. How can the company avoid Regional data transfer charges most cost-effectively?
choices: Launch one NAT gateway per Availability Zone.
Replace the NAT gateway with a NAT instance.
Deploy a gateway VPC endpoint for Amazon S3.
Use an EC2 Dedicated Host.
questions.explanation: A gateway VPC endpoint for S3 keeps S3 traffic on the AWS network path without NAT gateway data processing and cross-AZ transfer costs.

questions.question: An on-premises application generates large time-sensitive backup data to S3, and internet bandwidth is constrained for internal users. What long-term solution should be used?
choices: Use AWS VPN and proxy traffic through a VPC endpoint.
Establish AWS Direct Connect and route backup traffic through it.
Use daily AWS Snowball devices.
Request S3 service limit increases.
questions.explanation: Direct Connect provides dedicated network capacity to AWS for predictable high-throughput transfers with less impact on public internet links.

questions.question: A company must protect critical S3 data from accidental deletion. Which two actions should be taken?
choices: Enable versioning on the S3 bucket.
Enable MFA Delete on the S3 bucket.
Create a bucket policy.
Enable default encryption.
Create a lifecycle policy.
questions.explanation: Versioning preserves prior object versions, and MFA Delete adds strong protection for version deletion/suspension operations.

questions.question: An ingestion workflow uses SNS notifications and a Lambda function. Occasional connectivity failures cause missed ingestions unless rerun manually. Which two actions ensure all data is ingested in the future?
choices: Deploy the Lambda function in multiple Availability Zones.
Create an SQS queue and subscribe it to the SNS topic.
Increase Lambda CPU and memory.
Increase Lambda provisioned throughput.
Modify Lambda to read from the SQS queue.
questions.explanation: Adding SQS between SNS and Lambda provides durable buffering and retry behavior, reducing message loss during transient failures.

questions.question: Stores upload large files through SFTP, and some files unexpectedly contain PII. The company needs administrator alerts and remediation with the least development effort. Which solution should be used?
choices: Use S3 + Amazon Inspector scan + lifecycle deletion.
Use S3 + Amazon Macie scan + SNS notifications to administrators.
Build custom Lambda PII scanners + SNS notifications.
Build custom Lambda PII scanners + SES + lifecycle deletion.
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie provides managed sensitive-data discovery for S3 with minimal custom development, and SNS can notify administrators for response workflows.

questions.question: A company needs guaranteed EC2 capacity in three specific AZs in one Region for a 1-week event. What should it do?
choices: Purchase Regional Reserved Instances.
Create an On-Demand Capacity Reservation for the Region.
Purchase Reserved Instances for the Region and three AZs.
Create an On-Demand Capacity Reservation specifying the Region and three AZs.
questions.explanation: On-Demand Capacity Reservations guarantee capacity in specific Availability Zones for the required duration.

questions.question: A website stores its catalog on EC2 instance store. The company needs high availability and durable storage while preserving file-style access. What should be done?
choices: Move catalog to ElastiCache for Redis.
Use a larger EC2 instance with larger instance store.
Move catalog to S3 Glacier Deep Archive.
Move catalog to Amazon EFS.
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS provides durable, shared, multi-AZ file storage and preserves standard file system semantics for application access.

questions.question: Call transcript files are accessed randomly within 1 year, then infrequently after 1 year. Fast retrieval is required for <1-year files, and delayed retrieval is acceptable for older files. Which is most cost-effective?
choices: Store files in S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval with tags and query tags.
Store files in S3 Intelligent-Tiering, move to Glacier Flexible Retrieval after 1 year, use Athena and Glacier Select.
Store files and metadata in S3 Standard, then move files to Glacier Instant Retrieval after 1 year.
Store files in S3 Standard, move to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year, and store search metadata in Amazon RDS.
questions.explanation: Keeping recent data in S3 Standard ensures fast access, and transitioning older data to Deep Archive minimizes long-term cost when slower retrieval is acceptable.

questions.question: A production workload runs on 1,000 EC2 Linux instances with third-party software that needs urgent patching. What should a solutions architect do to patch as quickly as possible?
choices: Create a Lambda function to patch all instances.
Use Systems Manager Patch Manager.
Schedule a Systems Manager maintenance window.
Use Systems Manager Run Command with a custom patch command.
questions.explanation: Run Command can execute immediate custom patch commands at scale across managed instances, including third-party software remediation.

questions.question: An application must generate an easy-to-read HTML shipping report from a REST API and email it to multiple recipients every morning. Which two actions should be taken?
choices: Send application data to Kinesis Data Firehose.
Use Amazon SES to format and send the report by email.
Use a scheduled EventBridge rule to invoke an AWS Glue job that queries the API.
Use a scheduled EventBridge rule to invoke a Lambda function that queries the API.
Store data in S3 and use SNS as S3 event destination to email reports.
questions.explanation: EventBridge scheduled invocation plus Lambda is a lightweight way to generate daily reports, and SES is the managed service for sending formatted emails.

questions.question: An on-prem application outputs files from tens of GB to hundreds of TB. Data must be in a standard file system, highly available, auto-scaling, and low operational overhead. Which migration approach is best?
choices: Run containers on ECS and use S3.
Run containers on EKS and use EBS.
Run on EC2 in a Multi-AZ Auto Scaling group and use EFS.
Run on EC2 in a Multi-AZ Auto Scaling group and use EBS.
questions.explanation: EFS provides elastic shared file system storage across AZs and fits standard file system access with minimal storage administration.

questions.question: Accounting records must be immediately accessible for 1 year, archived for 9 more years, and undeletable by anyone (including root/admin) for the full 10 years with maximum resiliency. Which solution meets this?
choices: Store in S3 Glacier for 10 years and deny deletions with access policy.
Use S3 Intelligent-Tiering and deny deletions with IAM policy.
Lifecycle from S3 Standard to Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year, with S3 Object Lock in compliance mode for 10 years.
Lifecycle from S3 Standard to One Zone-IA after 1 year, with S3 Object Lock in governance mode for 10 years.
questions.explanation: S3 Object Lock compliance mode enforces WORM protection even against root users, and Glacier Deep Archive reduces long-term archive cost with high durability.

questions.question: Employees use Windows file shares currently hosted on two EC2 instances with duplicate data sync. The company wants a highly available, durable solution while preserving current user access patterns. What should be done?
choices: Migrate all data to Amazon S3 with IAM-authenticated access.
Set up an S3 File Gateway and mount it on the EC2 instances.
Migrate to Amazon FSx for Windows File Server with Multi-AZ.
Migrate to Amazon EFS with Multi-AZ.
questions.explanation: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server provides native SMB-compatible Windows file shares with Multi-AZ high availability and durability.

questions.question: A VPC has public, private, and database subnets in two AZs. Only EC2 instances in private subnets should access Amazon RDS instances. Which solution should be used?
choices: Associate DB subnets with a route table excluding public subnet CIDRs.
Attach a DB security group that denies inbound traffic from public-subnet instance security groups.
Attach a DB security group that allows inbound traffic only from private-subnet instance security groups.
Create peering connections between public/private and private/database subnets.
questions.explanation: Security groups are the correct control for instance-level database access; allowing inbound only from private-tier instance security groups enforces the requirement.

questions.question: A company uses Amazon API Gateway in ca-central-1 and wants to expose the API with its own domain name and HTTPS using a certificate. What should the company do?
choices: Use API Gateway stage variables and import the certificate into ACM
Create Route 53 records and import the certificate into ACM in us-east-1
Create a Regional API Gateway custom domain, import the certificate into ACM in the same Region, and configure Route 53
Create a Regional API Gateway custom domain, import the certificate into ACM in us-east-1, and configure Route 53
questions.explanation: For a Regional API Gateway endpoint, the ACM certificate must be in the same Region and attached to a custom domain name, with Route 53 routing traffic.

questions.question: A social media website allows users to upload images. The company wants to detect inappropriate content with minimal development effort. What should be done?
choices: Use Amazon Comprehend with human review
Use Amazon Rekognition with human review
Use Amazon SageMaker with Ground Truth
Deploy a custom ML model on AWS Fargate
questions.explanation: Amazon Rekognition provides built-in image moderation APIs, minimizing development effort.

questions.question: A company wants to run critical applications in containers without managing the underlying infrastructure. What is the best solution?
choices: Run Docker on Amazon EC2
Use Amazon ECS on EC2
Use Amazon ECS on AWS Fargate
Use ECS-optimized AMIs on EC2
questions.explanation: AWS Fargate is serverless for containers and removes the need to manage infrastructure.

questions.question: A company needs to process more than 30 TB of clickstream data daily from hundreds of global websites. What is the best solution?
choices: AWS Data Pipeline with EMR
EC2 Auto Scaling with S3 and Redshift
CloudFront with Lambda processing
Kinesis Data Streams with Firehose to S3 and Redshift
questions.explanation: Kinesis Data Streams and Firehose are designed for large-scale, real-time data ingestion and analytics.

questions.question: A website behind an Application Load Balancer must redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS. What should be done?
choices: Update network ACLs to allow only HTTPS
Replace HTTP with HTTPS in URLs
Create an ALB listener rule to redirect HTTP to HTTPS
Replace the ALB with a Network Load Balancer
questions.explanation: ALB listener rules support HTTP-to-HTTPS redirection natively.

questions.question: A company must store database credentials securely and rotate them automatically with minimal operational overhead. What is the best solution?
choices: Store credentials in instance metadata
Store credentials in encrypted S3 files
Store credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation
Store credentials in SSM Parameter Store with rotation
questions.explanation: AWS Secrets Manager natively supports automatic secret rotation with minimal effort.

questions.question: A public web application behind an ALB must use an SSL/TLS certificate issued by an external CA and be rotated yearly. What should be done?
choices: Use ACM to issue and auto-renew the certificate
Import the certificate into ACM and use managed renewal
Use ACM Private CA with auto-renewal
Import the certificate into ACM and manually rotate it using expiration notifications
questions.explanation: Externally issued certificates can be imported into ACM, but renewal must be handled manually.

questions.question: A company needs to convert uploaded PDF files (5 MB) to JPG images at scale and store both versions cost-effectively. What is the best solution?
choices: Store PDFs in S3 and use Lambda triggered by S3 events
Store PDFs in DynamoDB and use DynamoDB Streams
Use Elastic Beanstalk with EC2 and EBS
Use Elastic Beanstalk with EC2 and EFS
questions.explanation: S3 event notifications with Lambda provide a scalable, serverless, and cost-effective solution.

questions.question: A company is migrating Windows file servers to AWS and needs low-latency access from both on-premises and AWS with minimal changes. What should be done?
choices: Use FSx for Windows File Server only in AWS
Use S3 File Gateway only
Use S3 directly with File Gateway
Use FSx for Windows File Server with FSx File Gateway
questions.explanation: FSx File Gateway provides low-latency access on premises while integrating seamlessly with FSx in AWS.

questions.question: A hospital needs to detect PHI in PDF and JPEG reports uploaded through API Gateway and Lambda with minimal effort. What is the best solution?
choices: Use custom Python libraries
Use Textract and SageMaker
Use Textract and Amazon Comprehend Medical
Use Rekognition and Comprehend Medical
questions.explanation: Textract extracts text from documents, and Comprehend Medical is purpose-built for PHI detection.

questions.question: Files are stored in Amazon S3 for 4 years. They are frequently accessed for 30 days and rarely afterward, but must remain immediately accessible. What is the most cost-effective solution?
choices: Move to S3 Glacier after 30 days
Move to S3 One Zone-IA after 30 days
Move to S3 Standard-IA after 30 days
Move to S3 Standard-IA after 30 days and Glacier after 4 years
questions.explanation: S3 Standard-IA keeps data immediately accessible while reducing storage costs.

questions.question: An application processes messages from SQS and occasionally writes duplicate records to RDS. What should be done to ensure messages are processed only once?
choices: Create a new queue
Add queue permissions
Set a wait time for ReceiveMessage
Increase the visibility timeout
questions.explanation: An insufficient visibility timeout can cause messages to be processed more than once.

questions.question: A hybrid architecture requires low latency and high availability, with reduced cost and acceptable slower failover. What is the best solution?
choices: Direct Connect with VPN backup
VPN with dual tunnels
Dual Direct Connect connections
Direct Connect with automatic CLI failover
questions.explanation: Direct Connect provides low latency, while VPN as backup minimizes cost.

questions.question: A web application runs on EC2 with an ALB and uses Aurora PostgreSQL in a single AZ. The company wants high availability with minimal data loss and effort. What should be done?
choices: Multi-Region EC2 and Aurora replication
Multi-AZ Auto Scaling and Multi-AZ Aurora with RDS Proxy
Single-AZ with frequent snapshots
Multi-Region Auto Scaling with S3-based writes
questions.explanation: Multi-AZ for both compute and database provides high availability with minimal operational effort.

questions.question: An HTTP application behind a Network Load Balancer does not detect HTTP errors. The company wants better availability without custom code. What should be done?
choices: Enable HTTP health checks on the NLB
Add cron jobs to EC2 instances
Replace the NLB with an ALB and configure health checks and Auto Scaling
Create CloudWatch alarms on UnhealthyHostCount
questions.explanation: Application Load Balancers support HTTP health checks and work with Auto Scaling to replace unhealthy instances.

questions.question: A company runs a shopping application that uses Amazon DynamoDB to store customer information. In case of data corruption, a solutions architect needs to design a solution that meets an RPO of 15 minutes and an RTO of 1 hour. What should the solutions architect recommend?
choices: Configure DynamoDB global tables and point the application to a different AWS Region
Configure DynamoDB point-in-time recovery and restore to the desired point in time
Export DynamoDB data to Amazon S3 Glacier daily and restore from Glacier
Schedule Amazon EBS snapshots for the DynamoDB table every 15 minutes

questions.question: A company runs a photo processing application that frequently uploads and downloads pictures from Amazon S3 buckets in the same Region. Data transfer costs are high. How can the solutions architect reduce these costs?
choices: Route S3 calls through Amazon API Gateway
Deploy a NAT gateway with an endpoint policy for S3
Route traffic through an internet gateway
Deploy an S3 VPC gateway endpoint

questions.question: A company uses a bastion host in a public subnet and application servers in private subnets. Which security group changes are required to allow secure access from on premises? (Choose two)
choices: Allow inbound access to bastion host only from application instances
Allow inbound access to bastion host only from internal IP range
Allow inbound access to bastion host only from external IP range
Allow SSH access to application instances only from bastion private IP
Allow SSH access to application instances only from bastion public IP

questions.question: A two-tier web application has a public web tier and a private database tier. Which security group rules should be configured? (Choose two)
choices: Allow inbound HTTPS traffic to the web tier from 0.0.0.0/0
Allow outbound HTTPS traffic from the web tier to 0.0.0.0/0
Allow inbound SQL traffic to the database tier from the web tier security group
Allow outbound traffic from database tier to web tier
Allow inbound HTTPS and SQL traffic to the database tier from the web tier

questions.question: A multi-tier application drops transactions when tiers are overloaded. The company wants a modern, loosely coupled solution. What is MOST operationally efficient?
choices: Use API Gateway, Lambda, and SQS between services
Resize EC2 instances based on CloudWatch metrics
Use SNS between EC2 Auto Scaling groups
Use SQS between EC2 Auto Scaling groups and scale on queue length

questions.question: A company sends 10 TB/day of sensitive JSON data from on premises to Amazon S3 for near-real-time analytics. Which solution is MOST reliable?
choices: AWS DataSync over the public internet
AWS DataSync over AWS Direct Connect
AWS DMS over the public internet
AWS DMS over AWS Direct Connect

questions.question: A company needs real-time data ingestion with an API, stream processing, and storage with the least operational overhead. Which solution meets these requirements?
choices: EC2 API → Kinesis → Firehose → Lambda → S3
EC2 API → AWS Glue → S3
API Gateway → Kinesis → Firehose → Lambda → S3
API Gateway → Glue → Lambda → S3

questions.question: A company must retain DynamoDB data for 7 years with minimal operational effort. What is the BEST solution?
choices: Use DynamoDB point-in-time recovery
Use AWS Backup with retention policies
Manually back up DynamoDB to S3 with lifecycle rules
Use Lambda with EventBridge to back up DynamoDB

questions.question: A DynamoDB table has unpredictable evening traffic spikes and is mostly idle in the mornings. What is the BEST cost-optimized option?
choices: On-demand capacity mode
Global secondary index
Provisioned capacity with auto scaling
Provisioned capacity with global tables

questions.question: A company needs to securely share an encrypted AMI with an MSP Partner’s AWS account. What is the MOST secure approach?
choices: Make the AMI public and share the KMS key
Share the AMI privately and allow the MSP account to use the KMS key
Re-encrypt the AMI with the MSP’s KMS key
Export the AMI to S3 and re-import it in the MSP account

questions.question: A stateless processor application must scale based on workload and remain loosely coupled. Which design is BEST?
choices: SNS + Auto Scaling based on CPU
SQS + Auto Scaling based on network usage
SQS + Auto Scaling based on queue depth
SNS + Auto Scaling based on message count

questions.question: A company needs notification 30 days before ACM certificates expire. What should be done?
choices: Configure ACM to publish SNS notifications
Use AWS Config with EventBridge and SNS
Use Trusted Advisor with CloudWatch alarms
Use EventBridge rule and Lambda to send SNS alerts

questions.question: A US-hosted on-premises website is launching in Europe and needs faster load times immediately. What should be recommended?
choices: Migrate the site to EC2 in us-east-1
Move the site to S3 with replication
Use Amazon CloudFront with on-premises origin
Use Route 53 geoproximity routing

questions.question: A company wants to reduce EC2 costs for a three-tier architecture with predictable production usage and stoppable dev/test instances. What is MOST cost-effective?
choices: Spot for production, Reserved for dev/test
Reserved for production, On-Demand for dev/test
Spot blocks for production, Reserved for dev/test
On-Demand for production, Spot blocks for dev/test

questions.question: A company must ensure uploaded documents cannot be modified or deleted after storage. What is the BEST solution?
choices: Use S3 with Versioning and Object Lock
Use S3 Lifecycle policies
Use S3 Versioning and read-only ACLs
Use Amazon EFS mounted as read-only

questions.question: A company has several web servers that need to frequently access a common Amazon RDS MySQL Multi-AZ DB instance. The company wants a secure method for the web servers to connect to the database while meeting a security requirement to rotate user credentials frequently. Which solution meets these requirements?
choices: Store the database user credentials in AWS Secrets Manager. Grant the necessary IAM permissions to allow the web servers to access AWS Secrets Manager.
Store the database user credentials in AWS Systems Manager OpsCenter. Grant the necessary IAM permissions to allow the web servers to access OpsCenter.
Store the database user credentials in a secure Amazon S3 bucket. Grant the necessary IAM permissions to allow the web servers to retrieve credentials and access the database.
Store the database user credentials in files encrypted with AWS KMS on the web server file system. The web server should be able to decrypt the files and access the database.
questions.explanation: AWS Secrets Manager supports secure storage and automatic rotation of database credentials with minimal effort.

questions.question: A company hosts an application on AWS Lambda functions that are invoked by an Amazon API Gateway API. The Lambda functions save customer data to an Amazon Aurora MySQL database. Whenever the company upgrades the database, the Lambda functions fail to establish database connections until the upgrade is complete. The result is that customer data is not recorded for some events. Which solution will meet these requirements?
choices: Provision an Amazon RDS Proxy to sit between the Lambda functions and the database. Configure the Lambda functions to connect to the RDS proxy.
Increase the run time of the Lambda functions to the maximum. Create a retry mechanism in the code that stores the customer data in the database.
Persist the customer data to Lambda local storage. Configure new Lambda functions to scan the local storage to save the customer data to the database.
Store the customer data in an Amazon SQS FIFO queue. Create a new Lambda function that polls the queue and stores the customer data in the database.
questions.explanation: Using SQS decouples the system and ensures data is not lost during database downtime. Messages can be processed later.

questions.question: A survey company has gathered data for several years in an Amazon S3 bucket (3 TB). The company shares the data with a European firm and wants to minimize data transfer costs. Which solution meets these requirements?
choices: Configure the Requester Pays feature on the company's S3 bucket.
Configure S3 Cross-Region Replication to the partner’s bucket.
Configure cross-account access so the partner can access the S3 bucket.
Use S3 Intelligent-Tiering and sync to the partner’s bucket.
questions.explanation: Requester Pays ensures the accessing party (European firm) pays for data transfer and request costs.

questions.question: A company uses Amazon S3 to store confidential audit documents. Managers are concerned about accidental deletion. What should be done?
choices: Enable versioning and MFA Delete on the S3 bucket.
Enable MFA on IAM users.
Add a lifecycle policy to deny delete actions.
Use KMS encryption and restrict access to the key.
questions.explanation: Versioning protects against deletion, and MFA Delete prevents accidental or unauthorized deletions.

questions.question: A company uses an RDS MySQL DB. A reporting script impacts performance. What solution has the least operational overhead?
choices: Convert to Multi-AZ.
Create a read replica and run the script on it.
Manually export data daily.
Use ElastiCache.
questions.explanation: Read replicas offload read traffic and improve performance with minimal management overhead.

questions.question: An EC2 application needs to access S3 without using the internet. What should be done?
choices: Configure an S3 gateway endpoint.
Create an S3 bucket in a private subnet.
Create an S3 bucket in the same region.
Use a NAT gateway.
questions.explanation: An S3 VPC gateway endpoint allows private access to S3 without internet traffic.

questions.question: A company wants secure S3 access from EC2 in a VPC. Which TWO steps are required?
choices: Configure a VPC gateway endpoint for S3.
Make the bucket public.
Create a bucket policy to allow access only from the VPC/application.
Store IAM credentials on EC2.
Use a NAT instance.
questions.explanation: Use a VPC endpoint for private access and restrict access via bucket policy.

questions.question: A company experiences latency during DB export to staging. What is the best solution?
choices: Aurora + mysqldump backup/restore.
Aurora + database cloning.
RDS Multi-AZ + standby for staging.
RDS + mysqldump.
questions.explanation: Aurora cloning is fast and does not impact production performance.

questions.question: Users upload files to S3 that require immediate processing. Demand varies. What is the best solution?
choices: Use EMR.
S3 → SQS → EC2.
S3 → SQS → Lambda → DynamoDB.
EventBridge → Kinesis → Lambda.
questions.explanation: S3 + SQS + Lambda is serverless, scalable, and low overhead.

questions.question: An RDS MySQL database needs to separate read and write traffic quickly. What should be done?
choices: Use Multi-AZ and read from primary.
Use Multi-AZ and read from standby.
Create read replicas with smaller capacity.
Create read replicas with same capacity.
questions.explanation: Read replicas should match capacity to handle production read load effectively.

choices: Users can terminate an EC2 instance in any AWS Region except us-east-1.
Users can terminate an EC2 instance with the IP address 10.100.100.1 in the us-east-1 Region.
Users can terminate an EC2 instance in the us-east-1 Region when the user's source IP is 10.100.100.254.
Users cannot terminate an EC2 instance in the us-east-1 Region when the user's source IP is 10.100.100.254.
questions.explanation: The policy allows termination of EC2 instances only from the specified IP range and only in the us-east-1 region, while denying all EC2 actions in other regions.

questions.question: A company needs Windows shared storage with AD integration. What solution is best?
choices: Amazon EFS.
Storage Gateway.
S3 mounted as volume.
Amazon FSx for Windows File Server.
questions.explanation: FSx for Windows provides native SMB support and AD integration.

questions.question: Users receive duplicate emails due to multiple Lambda invocations from SQS. What is the best fix?
choices: Enable long polling.
Use FIFO queue with deduplication.
Increase visibility timeout.
Delete message before processing.
questions.explanation: Increasing visibility timeout prevents messages from being processed multiple times before completion.

questions.question: A company needs managed storage accessible via Lustre clients. What solution should be used?
choices: Storage Gateway.
EC2 Windows file share.
EFS with Lustre.
FSx for Lustre.
questions.explanation: FSx for Lustre is a fully managed service that supports Lustre clients.

questions.question: An EC2 app needs secure real-time encryption/decryption of certificates and highly available storage. What is the best solution?
choices: Secrets Manager.
Lambda cryptography.
KMS + S3.
KMS + EBS.
questions.explanation: KMS provides secure encryption, and S3 offers highly durable and available storage.

questions.question:
A solutions architect is designing a VPC with public and private subnets. The VPC and subnets use IPv4 CIDR blocks. There is one public subnet and one private subnet in each of three Availability Zones (AZs) for high availability. An internet gateway is used to provide internet access for the public subnets. The private subnets require access to the internet to allow Amazon EC2 instances to download software updates.
What should the solutions architect do to enable Internet access for the private subnets?
choices: Create three NAT gateways, one for each public subnet in each AZ. Create a private route table for each AZ that forwards non-VPC traffic to the NAT gateway in its AZ.
Create three NAT instances, one for each private subnet in each AZ. Create a private route table for each AZ that forwards non-VPC traffic to the NAT instance in its AZ.
Create a second internet gateway on one of the private subnets. Update the route table for the private subnets that forward non-VPC traffic to the private internet gateway.
Create an egress-only internet gateway on one of the public subnets. Update the route table for the private subnets that forward non-VPC traffic to the egress-only Internet gateway.
questions.explanation: NAT Gateways allow outbound internet access for private subnets without exposing them to inbound traffic. Deploying one NAT Gateway per AZ ensures high availability and avoids cross-AZ traffic.

questions.question:
A company wants to migrate an on-premises data center to AWS. The data center hosts an SFTP server that stores its data on an NFS-based file system. The server holds 200 GB of data that needs to be transferred. The server must be hosted on an Amazon EC2 instance that uses an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system.
Which combination of steps should a solutions architect take to automate this task? (Choose two.)
choices: Launch the EC2 instance into the same Availability Zone as the EFS file system.
Install an AWS DataSync agent in the on-premises data center.
Create a secondary Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume on the EC2 instance for the data.
Manually use an operating system copy command to push the data to the EC2 instance.
Use AWS DataSync to create a suitable location configuration for the on-premises SFTP server.
questions.explanation: AWS DataSync automates secure data transfer. Installing the DataSync agent on premises and configuring an SFTP location enables efficient migration to Amazon EFS.

questions.question:
A company has an AWS Glue extract, transform, and load (ETL) job that runs every day at the same time. The job processes XML data that is in an Amazon S3 bucket. New data is added to the S3 bucket every day. A solutions architect notices that AWS Glue is processing all the data during each run.
What should the solutions architect do to prevent AWS Glue from reprocessing old data?
choices: Edit the job to use job bookmarks.
Edit the job to delete data after the data is processed.
Edit the job by setting the NumberOfWorkers field to 1.
Use a FindMatches machine learning (ML) transform.
questions.explanation: AWS Glue job bookmarks keep track of processed data so that only new data is handled in subsequent job runs.

questions.question:
A solutions architect must design a highly available infrastructure for a website. The website is powered by Windows web servers that run on Amazon EC2 instances. The solutions architect must implement a solution that can mitigate a large-scale DDoS attack that originates from thousands of IP addresses. Downtime is not acceptable for the website.
Which actions should the solutions architect take to protect the website from such an attack? (Choose two.)
choices: Use AWS Shield Advanced to stop the DDoS attack.
Configure Amazon GuardDuty to automatically block the attackers.
Configure the website to use Amazon CloudFront for both static and dynamic content.
Use an AWS Lambda function to automatically add attacker IP addresses to VPC network ACLs.
Use EC2 Spot Instances in an Auto Scaling group with a target tracking scaling policy that is set to 80% CPU utilization.
questions.explanation: AWS Shield Advanced provides managed DDoS protection, and CloudFront absorbs and distributes traffic at edge locations to protect the origin.

questions.question:
A company is preparing to deploy a new serverless workload. A solutions architect must use the principle of least privilege to configure permissions that will be used to run an AWS Lambda function. An Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule will invoke the function.
Which solution meets these requirements?
choices: Add an execution role to the function with lambda:InvokeFunction as the action and * as the principal.
Add an execution role to the function with lambda:InvokeFunction as the action and Service: lambda.amazonaws.com as the principal.
Add a resource-based policy to the function with lambda:* as the action and Service: events.amazonaws.com as the principal.
Add a resource-based policy to the function with lambda:InvokeFunction as the action and Service: events.amazonaws.com as the principal.
questions.explanation: EventBridge requires a resource-based policy on the Lambda function that allows only lambda:InvokeFunction from events.amazonaws.com, ensuring least privilege.

questions.question:
A company is preparing to store confidential data in Amazon S3. For compliance reasons, the data must be encrypted at rest. Encryption key usage must be logged for auditing purposes. Keys must be rotated every year.
Which solution meets these requirements and is the MOST operationally efficient?
choices: Server-side encryption with customer-provided keys (SSE-C)
Server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed keys (SSE-S3)
Server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS) with manual rotation
Server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS) with automatic rotation
questions.explanation: SSE-KMS provides audit logs via CloudTrail and supports automatic annual key rotation with minimal operational overhead.

questions.question:
A bicycle sharing company is developing a multi-tier architecture to track the location of its bicycles during peak operating hours. The company wants to use these data points in its existing analytics platform. A solutions architect must determine the most viable multi-tier option to support this architecture. The data points must be accessible from the REST API.
Which action meets these requirements for storing and retrieving location data?
choices: Use Amazon Athena with Amazon S3.
Use Amazon API Gateway with AWS Lambda.
Use Amazon QuickSight with Amazon Redshift.
Use Amazon API Gateway with Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics.
questions.explanation: API Gateway with Lambda provides a scalable REST API backend suitable for ingesting and retrieving real-time location data.

questions.question:
A company has an automobile sales website that stores its listings in a database on Amazon RDS. When an automobile is sold, the listing needs to be removed from the website and the data must be sent to multiple target systems.
Which design should a solutions architect recommend?
choices: Create an AWS Lambda function triggered when the database on Amazon RDS is updated to send the information to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue for the targets to consume.
Create an AWS Lambda function triggered when the database on Amazon RDS is updated to send the information to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO queue for the targets to consume.

Subscribe to an RDS event notification and send an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue fanned out to multiple Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topics. Use AWS Lambda functions to update the targets.

Subscribe to an RDS event notification and send an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic fanned out to multiple Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queues. Use AWS Lambda functions to update the targets.
questions.explanation: SNS enables fan-out messaging to multiple SQS queues so each target system can process the event independently.

questions.question:
A company needs to store data in Amazon S3 and must prevent the data from being changed. The company wants new objects that are uploaded to Amazon S3 to remain unchangeable for a nonspecific amount of time until the company decides to modify the objects. Only specific users in the company's AWS account can have the ability to delete the objects.
What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
choices: Create an S3 Glacier vault. Apply a write-once, read-many (WORM) vault lock policy to the objects.
Create an S3 bucket with S3 Object Lock enabled. Enable versioning. Set a retention period of 100 years. Use governance mode as the S3 bucket’s default retention mode for new objects.
Create an S3 bucket. Use AWS CloudTrail to track any S3 API events that modify the objects. Upon notification, restore the modified objects from any backup versions that the company has.

Create an S3 bucket with S3 Object Lock enabled. Enable versioning. Add a legal hold to the objects. Add the s3:PutObjectLegalHold permission to the IAM policies of users who need to delete the objects.
questions.explanation: Legal holds prevent object modification indefinitely until explicitly removed by authorized users, meeting compliance and access control requirements.

questions.question:
A social media company allows users to upload images to its website. The website runs on Amazon EC2 instances. During upload requests, the website resizes the images to a standard size and stores the resized images in Amazon S3. Users are experiencing slow upload requests to the website.
The company needs to reduce coupling within the application and improve website performance. A solutions architect must design the most operationally efficient process for image uploads.
Which combination of actions should the solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
choices: Configure the application to upload images to S3 Glacier.
Configure the web server to upload the original images to Amazon S3.
Configure the application to upload images directly from each user's browser to Amazon S3 through the use of a presigned URL
Configure S3 Event Notifications to invoke an AWS Lambda function when an image is uploaded. Use the function to resize the image.
Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule that invokes an AWS Lambda function on a schedule to resize uploaded images
questions.explanation: Presigned URLs offload uploads directly to S3, reducing load on EC2. S3 event notifications trigger Lambda for asynchronous image resizing, improving performance and decoupling.

questions.question: A company recently migrated a message processing system to AWS. The system receives messages into an ActiveMQ queue running on an Amazon EC2 instance. Messages are processed by a consumer application running on Amazon EC2. The consumer application processes the messages and writes results to a MySQL database running on Amazon EC2. The company wants this application to be highly available with low operational complexity. Which architecture offers the HIGHEST availability?
choices: A. Add a second ActiveMQ server to another Availability Zone. Add an additional consumer EC2 instance in another Availability Zone. Replicate the MySQL database to another Availability Zone.

B. Use Amazon MQ with active/standby brokers con gured across two Availability Zones. Add an additional consumer EC2 instance in another Availability Zone. Replicate the MySQL database to another Availability Zone.

C. Use Amazon MQ with active/standby brokers con gured across two Availability Zones. Add an additional consumer EC2 instance in another Availability Zone. Use Amazon RDS for MySQL with Multi-AZ enabled.

D. Use Amazon MQ with active/standby brokers con gured across two Availability Zones. Add an Auto Scaling group for the consumer EC2 instances across two Availability Zones. Use Amazon RDS for MySQL with Multi-AZ enabled.
questions.explanation: Fully managed services + Multi-AZ + Auto Scaling provides highest availability with low ops.

questions.question: A company hosts a containerized web application on a eet of on-premises servers that process incoming requests. The number of requests is growing quickly. The on-premises servers cannot handle the increased number of requests. The company wants to move the application to AWS with minimum code changes and minimum development effort. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
choices: A. Use AWS Fargate on Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) to run the containerized web application with Service Auto Scaling. Use an Application Load Balancer to distribute the incoming requests.
B. Use two Amazon EC2 instances to host the containerized web application. Use an Application Load Balancer to distribute the incoming requests.
C. Use AWS Lambda with a new code that uses one of the supported languages. Create multiple Lambda functions to support the load. Use Amazon API Gateway as an entry point to the Lambda functions.
D. Use a high performance computing (HPC) solution such as AWS ParallelCluster to establish an HPC cluster that can process the incoming requests at the appropriate scale.
questions.explanation: Fargate removes server management and supports containers directly.

questions.question: A company uses 50 TB of data for reporting. The company wants to move this data from on premises to AWS. The data center does not have any available network bandwidth. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
choices: A. Use AWS DataSync to move the data. Create a custom transformation job by using AWS Glue.
B. Order an AWS Snowcone device to move the data. Deploy the transformation application to the device.
C. Order an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device. Copy the data to the device. Create a custom transformation job by using AWS Glue.
D. Order an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device that includes Amazon EC2 compute. Copy the data to the device. Create a new EC2 instance on AWS to run the transformation application.
questions.explanation: Snowball Edge with compute allows local processing and avoids bandwidth limits.

questions.question: A company has created an image analysis application with growing demand and variable load. Which solution meets these requirements?
choices: A. Use AWS Lambda to process the photos. Store the photos and metadata in DynamoDB.
B. Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to process the photos and to store the photos and metadata.
C. Use AWS Lambda to process the photos. Store the photos in Amazon S3. Retain DynamoDB to store the metadata.
D. Increase EC2 instances and use EBS.
questions.explanation: Lambda + S3 + DynamoDB is scalable and serverless.

questions.question: A company wants private network access from EC2 to S3. Which change should be made?
choices: A. Create a NAT gateway.
B. Restrict security group outbound.
C. Move EC2 to private subnets and create an S3 VPC endpoint.
D. Use Direct Connect.
questions.explanation: VPC endpoint ensures private connectivity to S3.

questions.question: A company wants a static, secure, scalable website with minimal overhead. (Choose two.)
choices: A. Con gure Amazon CloudFront in front of the website to use HTTPS functionality.
B. Deploy AWS WAF for HTTPS.
C. Use Lambda to serve content.
D. Use S3 static website hosting.
E. Use EC2 Auto Scaling.
questions.explanation: S3 + CloudFront is low-cost, scalable, and secure.

questions.question: A company wants to stream CloudWatch Logs to OpenSearch in near real time. What is the best solution?
choices: A. Configure a CloudWatch Logs subscription.
B. Use Lambda.
C. Use Kinesis Firehose.
D. Use Kinesis Agent.
questions.explanation: Kinesis Firehose integrates natively with OpenSearch with minimal ops.

questions.question: A company needs scalable and cost-effective storage for 900 TB of documents. What is the best solution?
choices: A. Amazon EBS
B. Amazon EFS
C. Amazon OpenSearch Service
D. Amazon S3
questions.explanation: S3 is the most cost-effective and scalable storage solution.

questions.question: A company wants to protect APIs across multiple accounts from SQL injection and XSS with least admin effort. What is the best solution?
choices: A. Use AWS WAF in each region.
B. Use AWS Firewall Manager with WAF.
C. Use AWS Shield with WAF.
D. Use AWS Shield in one region.
questions.explanation: Firewall Manager centrally manages WAF across accounts.

questions.question: A company has NLBs in two regions and wants to route traffic globally with high performance. What should they use?
choices: A. Route 53 geolocation + CloudFront.
B. AWS Global Accelerator.
C. Route 53 + Elastic IPs + CloudFront.
D. ALB + Route 53 latency + CloudFront.
questions.explanation: Global Accelerator improves global performance and availability.

questions.question:
A company is running an online transaction processing (OLTP) workload on AWS. This workload uses an unencrypted Amazon RDS DB instance in a Multi-AZ deployment. Daily database snapshots are taken from this instance.
What should a solutions architect do to ensure the database and snapshots are always encrypted moving forward?
choices: Encrypt a copy of the latest DB snapshot. Replace existing DB instance by restoring the encrypted snapshot.
Create a new encrypted Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume and copy the snapshots to it. Enable encryption on the DB instance.
Copy the snapshots and enable encryption using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Restore encrypted snapshot to an existing DB instance.
Copy the snapshots to an Amazon S3 bucket that is encrypted using server-side encryption with AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) managed keys (SSE-KMS).
questions.explanation: Amazon RDS encryption cannot be enabled on an existing unencrypted DB instance. The correct approach is to copy and encrypt a snapshot, then restore it to create a new encrypted DB instance. All future snapshots will also be encrypted.

questions.question:
A company wants to build a scalable key management infrastructure to support developers who need to encrypt data in their applications.
What should a solutions architect do to reduce the operational burden?
choices: Use multi-factor authentication (MFA) to protect the encryption keys.
Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) to protect the encryption keys.
Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to create, store, and assign the encryption keys.
Use an IAM policy to limit the scope of users who have access permissions to protect the encryption keys.
questions.explanation: AWS KMS is a fully managed, scalable key management service that handles key creation, storage, rotation, and access control, significantly reducing operational overhead.

questions.question:
A company has a dynamic web application hosted on two Amazon EC2 instances. The company has its own SSL certificate, which is on each instance to perform SSL termination.
There has been an increase in traffic recently, and the operations team determined that SSL encryption and decryption is causing the compute capacity of the web servers to reach their maximum limit.
What should a solutions architect do to increase the application's performance?
choices: Create a new SSL certificate using AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Install the ACM certificate on each instance.
Create an Amazon S3 bucket. Migrate the SSL certificate to the S3 bucket. Configure the EC2 instances to reference the bucket for SSL termination.
Create another EC2 instance as a proxy server. Migrate the SSL certificate to the new instance and configure it to direct connections to the existing EC2 instances.
Import the SSL certificate into AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an Application Load Balancer with an HTTPS listener that uses the SSL certificate from ACM.
questions.explanation: Using an Application Load Balancer for SSL termination offloads encryption and decryption from the EC2 instances, improving application performance and scalability.

questions.question:
A company has a highly dynamic batch processing job that uses many Amazon EC2 instances to complete it. The job is stateless in nature, can be started and stopped at any given time with no negative impact, and typically takes upwards of 60 minutes total to complete.
The company has asked a solutions architect to design a scalable and cost-effective solution that meets the requirements of the job.
What should the solutions architect recommend?
choices: Implement EC2 Spot Instances.
Purchase EC2 Reserved Instances.
Implement EC2 On-Demand Instances.
Implement the processing on AWS Lambda.
questions.explanation: Spot Instances are ideal for stateless, interruptible workloads and offer significant cost savings compared to On-Demand or Reserved Instances.

questions.question:
A company runs its two-tier ecommerce website on AWS. The web tier consists of a load balancer that sends traffic to Amazon EC2 instances. The database tier uses an Amazon RDS DB instance.
The EC2 instances and the RDS DB instance should not be exposed to the public internet. The EC2 instances require internet access to complete payment processing of orders through a third-party web service. The application must be highly available.
Which combination of configuration options will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
choices: Use an Auto Scaling group to launch the EC2 instances in private subnets. Deploy an RDS Multi-AZ DB instance in private subnets.
Configure a VPC with two private subnets and two NAT gateways across two Availability Zones. Deploy an Application Load Balancer in the private subnets.
Use an Auto Scaling group to launch the EC2 instances in public subnets across two Availability Zones. Deploy an RDS Multi-AZ DB instance in private subnets.
Configure a VPC with one public subnet, one private subnet, and two NAT gateways across two Availability Zones. Deploy an Application Load Balancer in the public subnet.
Configure a VPC with two public subnets, two private subnets, and two NAT gateways across two Availability Zones. Deploy an Application Load Balancer in the public subnets.
questions.explanation: Private subnets protect EC2 and RDS from public exposure, NAT gateways provide outbound internet access, and an Application Load Balancer in public subnets ensures high availability.

questions.question: A solutions architect needs to implement a solution to reduce a company's storage costs. All the company's data is in the Amazon S3 Standard storage class. The company must keep all data for at least 25 years. Data from the most recent 2 years must be highly available and immediately retrievable. Which solution will meet these requirements?
choices: A. Set up an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition objects to S3 Glacier Deep Archive immediately.
B. Set up an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition objects to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 2 years.
C. Use S3 Intelligent-Tiering. Activate the archiving option to ensure that data is archived in S3 Glacier Deep Archive.
D. Set up an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition objects to S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA) immediately and to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 2 years.
questions.explanation: Keep recent data in S3 Standard for immediate access, then transition older data to Glacier Deep Archive for long-term cost savings.

questions.question: A media company is evaluating the possibility of moving its systems to the AWS Cloud. The company needs at least 10 TB of storage with the maximum possible I/O performance for video processing, 300 TB of very durable storage for storing media content, and 900 TB of storage to meet requirements for archival media that is not in use anymore. Which set of services should a solutions architect recommend to meet these requirements?
choices: A. Amazon EBS for maximum performance, Amazon S3 for durable data storage, and Amazon S3 Glacier for archival storage
B. Amazon EBS for maximum performance, Amazon EFS for durable data storage, and Amazon S3 Glacier for archival storage
C. Amazon EC2 instance store for maximum performance, Amazon EFS for durable data storage, and Amazon S3 for archival storage
D. Amazon EC2 instance store for maximum performance, Amazon S3 for durable data storage, and Amazon S3 Glacier for archival storage
questions.explanation: EBS provides high IOPS, S3 offers durable storage, and Glacier is best for archival.

questions.question: A company wants to run applications in containers in the AWS Cloud. These applications are stateless and can tolerate disruptions within the underlying infrastructure. The company needs a solution that minimizes cost and operational overhead. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
choices: A. Use Spot Instances in an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group to run the application containers.
B. Use Spot Instances in an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) managed node group.
C. Use On-Demand Instances in an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group to run the application containers.
D. Use On-Demand Instances in an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) managed node group.
questions.explanation: Spot Instances reduce cost and are suitable for fault-tolerant stateless workloads with lower overhead than EKS.

questions.question: A company is running a multi-tier web application on premises. The web application is containerized and runs on Linux hosts connected to a PostgreSQL database. The operational overhead is limiting growth. Which combination of actions should the solutions architect take? (Choose two.)
choices: A. Migrate the PostgreSQL database to Amazon Aurora.
B. Migrate the web application to be hosted on Amazon EC2 instances.
C. Set up an Amazon CloudFront distribution for the web application content.
